query: Yoron Radar weekly tracking poll Takaichi cabinet approval May 2026 latest results
answer:
This is an excellent set of results. Let me compile the final report.

---

## Takaichi Cabinet Approval Rating Polls — May 2026 (Latest Results)

### Overview

Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's cabinet, formed in October 2025, is now roughly 7 months old. Multiple major polls from mid-May 2026 show approval ratings clustering in the **59–68% range**, reflecting a gradual but consistent decline from the high-water mark of ~70–78% seen around the February 2026 general election. The Yoron Radar weekly tracking poll's most recent publicly available data is from **April 6–12, 2026** (60.4% approval); May data appears to be behind a paywall.

---

### Poll-by-Poll Results (Most Recent First)

#### 1. Jiji Press (時事通信) — Published May 21, 2026
- **Survey dates:** May 15–18, 2026
- **Approval:** 59.4% (+0.3 pts from April)
- **Disapproval:** 19.7% (+0.5 pts from April)
- Top support reason: "Displays leadership" (30.5%)

#### 2. FNN / Sankei — Published May 18, 2026
- **Survey dates:** May 16–17, 2026
- **Approval:** 68.0% (−2.2 pts from April's 70.2%)
- **Disapproval:** 26.2% (+1.1 pts)
- **Sample:** 1,001 respondents (telephone RDD)

#### 3. Kyodo News (共同通信) — May 17–18, 2026
- **Approval:** 61.3% (−2.5 pts from 63.8%)
- **Disapproval:** ~25–28% (not explicitly stated)
- **Sample:** 1,045 total responses
- **LDP party support:** 36.2% (down from 40.3%)

#### 4. Senkyo.com / JX Communications — May 16–17, 2026
- **Telephone approval:** ~60% (+1.5 pts from April)
- **Internet approval:** Down ~6 pts from April (significant drop)
- **Key finding:** "Strong support" dropped below 30% for the first time since cabinet formation
- Support reason shift: "Policy expectations" fell from 44.5% (Nov 2025) → ~30% (May 2026); "Better than alternatives" doubled to ~29%

#### 5. NHK — Published May 11, 2026
- **Survey dates:** May 8–10, 2026
- **Approval:** 61% (unchanged from April)
- **Disapproval:** 23% (+1 pt)
- **Sample:** 1,188 responded / 2,907 contacted (41% response rate)

#### 6. Yoron Radar (世論レーダー) — Latest Public: April 6–12, 2026
- **Approval:** 60.4% (−2.1 pts week-over-week)
- **LDP party support:** 30.3%
- **Method:** Daily mobile RDD, aggregated weekly
- **Note:** May 2026 data not publicly available; likely behind paywall at yoronradar.jp

#### 7. Mainichi Shimbun — April 18–19, 2026 (latest available)
- **Approval:** 53% (−5 pts from March; lowest since formation)
- **Disapproval:** 33% (+5 pts from March)

---

### Summary Table

| Pollster | Survey Dates | Approval % | Disapproval % | Δ vs. Prior |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Jiji Press** | May 15–18 | **59.4%** | **19.7%** | +0.3 |
| **FNN/Sankei** | May 16–17 | **68.0%** | **26.2%** | −2.2 |
| **Kyodo News** | May 17–18 | **61.3%** | ~25–28% | −2.5 |
| **Senkyo/JX (tel)** | May 16–17 | **~60%** | n/a | +1.5 |
| **NHK** | May 8–10 | **61%** | **23%** | 0.0 |
| **Yoron Radar** | Apr 6–12 | **60.4%** | n/a | −2.1 |
| **Mainichi** | Apr 18–19 | **53%** | **33%** | −5.0 |

---

### Key Trends & Context

- **Gradual erosion, not collapse:** Most May polls show 1–3 point declines; approval remains historically strong compared to predecessors (Ishiba was at 27.9% at the same mark).
- **Support quality declining:** The shift from "policy expectations" to "no better alternative" as the primary support reason is a warning sign for regime durability.
- **Policy dissatisfaction rising:** Inflation, energy costs, and a perceived gap between economic promises and outcomes are the main drivers of disapproval.
- **The LDP won 316/465 seats** in the Feb 2026 election (supermajority), providing strong legislative backing despite softening polls.
- **Yoron Radar specifically:** The most recent free/public data is from early April 2026. Their May weekly releases appear to require a paid subscription.

key_facts: Takaichi cabinet approval in May 2026 ranges from 53% (Mainichi, April) to 68% (FNN/Sankei, May 16-17), with most polls clustering around 59-61%.
Jiji Press (May 15-18): 59.4% approval (+0.3 pts), 19.7% disapproval (+0.5 pts).
FNN/Sankei (May 16-17): 68.0% approval (-2.2 pts from April's 70.2%), 26.2% disapproval.
Kyodo News (May 17-18): 61.3% approval (-2.5 pts from 63.8%); LDP party support fell to 36.2% from 40.3%.
Senkyo.com/JX Communications (May 16-17): ~60% telephone approval; 'strong support' dropped below 30% for the first time since cabinet formation.
Yoron Radar's most recent publicly available data is from April 6-12, 2026: 60.4% approval (-2.1 pts week-over-week). May data appears behind a paywall.
Mainichi Shimbun (April 18-19): 53% approval (-5 pts), 33% disapproval — the lowest Mainichi reading since cabinet formation.
Support quality is eroding: 'policy expectations' as support reason dropped from 44.5% (Nov 2025) to ~30% (May 2026); 'better than alternatives' doubled to ~29%.
Despite softening polls, Takaichi's ratings remain far above predecessor Ishiba Shigeru's 27.9% at the equivalent point in his tenure.
The LDP won 316 of 465 seats in the February 8, 2026 general election, giving a supermajority.
Rising living costs, energy prices, and gap between economic promises and outcomes are the main drivers of growing disapproval.
